text.title: LangChain and LangGraph Known Failure Modes
text.summary: Concrete failure modes: deprecated patterns, async pitfalls, tool routing issues, and security considerations.

antipattern.description: Using LLMChain, AgentExecutor, ConversationChain, or SequentialChain from legacy LangChain.
antipattern.reason: These are deprecated but still widely documented on Stack Overflow. Agents trained on web data will suggest them. Use LCEL equivalents: `prompt | model | parser`, `create_react_agent`, `RunnableWithMessageHistory`, LCEL pipe chain.

antipattern.description: Using a custom `CallbackHandler` with blocking I/O in an async chain.
antipattern.reason: LangChain callbacks execute synchronously in async chains by default. A blocking callback stalls the entire chain. Use `AsyncCallbackHandler` instead.

antipattern.description: Using `batch()` with tools that are not thread-safe.
antipattern.reason: LCEL `batch()` runs in a thread pool by default. Non-thread-safe tools cause race conditions. Use `abatch()` with async tools instead.

antipattern.description: Providing ambiguous or overlapping tool descriptions without testing tool selection explicitly.
antipattern.reason: The LLM reads tool descriptions verbatim. Ambiguous descriptions cause the agent to select wrong tools without error. Wrong tool selection fails silently.

rule.statement: Sanitize user input before injecting into prompts; validate retrieved content before including in RAG chains to prevent prompt injection.
rule.rationale: A malicious document in the corpus or a crafted user input can override agent instructions. Retrieved content must be treated as untrusted data, not trusted instructions.

rule.statement: Disable LangSmith tracing (`LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=false`) in latency-critical paths; tracing adds ~30ms per chain call.
rule.rationale: At high throughput, 30ms per call compounds. In a 10-step chain, tracing adds 300ms — measurable in user-facing endpoints.
